q: In wave optics, the ____ principle explains how light propagates from a light source.
The difference in states of the periodic waves at the same instant is the difference ____?.
For a bright fringe, the path difference is equal to ____?
a: only odd integral number of wavelengths

q: The bending of waves around the corners of an obstacle is an example of of waves ___?
The bending of waves through an aperture into the region of the geometrical shadow of the obstacle is an example of of waves ____?
In a single slit diffraction, the bright and dark fringes are created by the diffracted ____ light waves.
In single slit diffraction, the width of the central maximum will be ____ if the wavelength of light increases.
In single-slit diffraction, the width of the central maximum will be if the screen distance is increased.
In single slit diffraction, the width of the central maximum will be ____ if the slit width is increased.
____ is an example of reflection gratings.
For a dark fringe, the path difference is equal to ____?
a: an odd integral number of wavelengths
an odd integral multiple of half-wavelength
